title: The Unreturning Ridge

features.name: final-camp
features.desc-on-touch: A cleared flat space where someone stopped climbing. Stones arranged in a circle, weathered beyond dating. No record of descent.

features.name: altitude-gauge
features.desc-on-touch: Air this thin tastes of silence. Your breath counts the distance. The ridge remembers who passed through and who stayed.

features.name: equipment-scatter
features.desc-on-touch: Pieces of old climbing gear, scattered as if left incrementally. Metal that remembers fingertips.

features.name: wind-carving
features.desc-on-touch: The rock has been shaped by pressure into something almost legible. If you read it, you'll know something about what altitude does to intention.

features.name: visibility-zone
features.desc-on-touch: From here you can see three other ridges that were never mapped. They look like they remember being one mountain once. The archive keeps the climb and the climbers equally.

features.name: silence-boundary
features.desc-on-touch: Beyond this point the wind stops carrying sound. It's not quiet—it's something wind does at this height. The climb continues into it.

inscription: at the ridge's throat, climbs and descents become grammatically indistinguishable

file.name: ClimbLog_RedactionsMountainCamp_1987_Onward.txt
file.note: A climber's journal where later entries are progressively redacted, as if altitude made them unwritable. The final page is blank except for an elevation number.

exits.label: back down the climb that still remembers reporting
north to the ridge's continuation where maps go uncertain
west along the ridge where pressure becomes weather
